title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa discusses production deployment of AI agents serving state and local governments. The talk covers agent architecture, A2A protocol, Effect-TS tooling, evaluation frameworks, long context handling, monitoring, and integration with Claude and Cursor.
writeup: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa walks through how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent system serving state and local governments in production. The talk covers the agent architecture, use of the A2A protocol, Effect-TS tooling, evaluation frameworks, long context handling, and continuous monitoring, along with integration with Claude and Cursor. It grounds agentic workflows and tool use in a regulated public-sector deployment rather than a demo setting, surfacing the engineering needed to keep agents reliable at scale.
